question.text: What is the registration fee payable by a Merchant Banker at the time of grant of certificate of registration by SEBI?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Every Merchant Banker is required to pay a sum of Rs. 20 lakhs as registration fees at the time of grant of certificate of registration. To keep the registration in force, Rs. 9 lakhs must be paid every three years from the sixth year. **The Trap:** Rs. 50 lakhs might seem right because the application fee is Rs. 50,000 and students may scale it up incorrectly. The actual registration fee is Rs. 20 lakhs. **Cross-Exam Context:** Registration fees are commonly tested across NISM exams. Merchant banker (Rs. 20 lakhs), Banker to Issue (Rs. 20 lakhs) - both have the same registration fee. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The renewal fee of Rs. 9 lakhs every three years must be paid one month before the expiry of the block for which the fee has been paid. Missing this deadline can lead to suspension.
